channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli military warns of operations on southern Lebanon's coast
item.description: Israel's military on Monday said it would soon launch operations on Lebanon’s southern coast, which would mark a further escalation of its fight against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Albania's opposition protests and demands a caretaker Cabinet
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Opposition supporters in Albania protested again Monday, demanding that the government be replaced by a technocratic caretaker Cabinet before next year’s parliamentary election.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tunisia's President Saied wins landslide reelection after cracking down on opponents
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — Tunisia’s President Kais Saied won a landslide reelection victory in results announced Monday after a campaign season that saw his opponents jailed alongside journalists, activists and attorneys.
item.dc:creator: BOUAZZA BEN BOUAZZA and ANNIE RISEMBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands survived a brutal gang attack in Haiti that killed 70. Now they face an uncertain future
item.description: PONT-SONDÉ, Haiti (AP) — Under the cover of night, dozens of gang members crept toward the small town of Pont-Sondé in central Haiti armed with knives and assault rifles as families slept.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE-RICHARD LUXAMA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US hits Hamas funding network with sanctions on attack anniversary
item.description: The Treasury Department announced Monday, the anniversary of Hamas's Oct. 7 attack on Israel, it would be sanctioning part of the Palestinian militant group’s funding network.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Kenya’s deputy president defends himself before impeachment
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya’s deputy president, facing an impeachment motion in which he's accused of supporting anti-government protests in June and of being involved in corruption and other irregularities, has defended himself and confirmed that he would appear before parliament on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about fighting in Lebanon and Gaza
item.description: Palestinian militants in Gaza and Lebanon fired waves of rockets into Israel as the nation mourned the anniversary of the Oct. 7 Hamas attack that killed 1,200 people and set off a war that has engulfed Gaza and parts of Lebanon and threatened to ignite a wider regional conflict.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tunisia's President Saied wins second term after cracking down on the opposition
item.description: Tunisia’s President Kais Saied has won re-election in a landslide victory after a campaign season that saw his opponents jailed alongside journalists, activists and attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Albania's opposition protests and demands a caretaker Cabinet
item.description: Opposition supporters in Albania have protested again and demanded that the government be replaced by a technocratic caretaker Cabinet before next year’s parliamentary election.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How many are dead, missing one year since Hamas' attack on Israel?
item.description: Oct. 7 marks the first anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel, which prompted an Israeli invasion of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine strikes a Russian oil hub as Zelenskyy says the war is in 'a very important phase'
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s military said it struck a major oil terminal Monday in Crimea that provides fuel for Russia’s war effort as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the war has entered a key phase.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's Churchill moment comes amid multifront war against Iranian terror, expert says
item.description: Netanyahu is facing his greatest challenge as Israel’s longest-serving Prime Minister while leading the Jewish state in an existential war against the county’s Islamist terrorist enemies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian arms dealer Viktor Bout, who was traded for Brittney Griner, to sell weapons to Iran-backed Houthis
item.description: Russian "Merchant of Death" Viktor Bout has reportedly resumed his role as an arms dealer following his December 2022 release from U.S. custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell praises Israel’s 'unshakeable resolve' one year after Oct. 7 attacks
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) praised Israel’s “unshakeable resolve” to mark the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks that left more than 1,100 people dead in Israeli communities outside of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: A top official with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's party quits, a year before the national election
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A top official with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's party announced his resignation on Monday, citing health problems, a year before an election in which the center-left Social Democrats face an uphill struggle, Kevin Kühnert, 35, has been the Social Democrats' general secretary — the
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The world's rivers faced the driest year in three decades in 2023, the UN weather agency says
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. weather agency is reporting that 2023 was the driest year in more than three decades for the world's rivers, as the record-hot year underpinned a drying up of water flows and contributed to prolonged droughts in some places.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas launches rockets from Gaza one year after Oct. 7 attacks, while IDF strikes terror targets
item.description: Hamas launched five rockets into Israel on the anniversary of the Oct. 7 terrorist attacks that started the war in Gaza, the Israel Defense Forces say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan and Georgia Arab American voters sour on Harris
item.description: Vice President Harris is facing growing signs that Arab American and Muslim voters are souring on her in the key battlegrounds of Michigan and Georgia as anger rises over the expanding conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, The Hill

item.title: Oct. 7 anniversary: 26 moments that defined a devastating year
item.description: Hamas shocked the world — and forever changed the Middle East — when hundreds of its fighters flooded into Israel on Oct. 7, massacring border communes, murdering revelers at a music festival and taking scores of people hostage.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Mozambique headed for crucial elections amid jihadist insurgency and drought-induced hunger
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambicans will vote this week for a new president who many hope will bring peace to an oil- and gas-rich northern province that has been ravaged by a jihadist insurgency for nearly seven years.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO and MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An explosion outside the Karachi, Pakistan, airport kills 2 workers from China and injures others
item.description: KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — A massive blast outside the Karachi airport in southern Pakistan killed two workers from China on Sunday and injured at least eight others in the latest deadly attack on Chinese in the country.
item.dc:creator: ADIL JAWAD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris is pressed on support for Netanyahu in '60 Minutes' preview
item.description: Vice President Harris was pressed on the U.S.’s support for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in an upcoming interview on CBS News’s “60 Minutes.” “We supply Israel with billions of dollars in military aid,” correspondent Bill Whitaker said in a clip highlighted by Mediaite.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Biden orders 500 more active-duty troops to North Carolina to aid in Helene recovery
item.description: President Biden ordered the Department of Defense to move an additional 500 active-duty troops into western North Carolina to aid in the recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene, the White House announced Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: New report finds nearly 200% increase in antisemitic incidents in US since Oct. 7 Hamas terror massacre
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League recorded around 10,000 antisemitic incidents since Oct. 7, marking the largest year-to-year increase since the group started recording such incidents in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rising from the ashes: Israelis in nation's war-torn south move home a year after October 7 massacre
item.description: The unique communal structure of the kibbutz plays a vital role in fostering resilience and support as people heal from the devastating impact of the October 7 massacre with many now returning home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military kills 250 Hezbollah terrorists since start of limited ground operation in Lebanon
item.description: Israel's military says it has killed 250 Hezbollah terrorists and has struck more than 2,000 military targets since it launched a limited ground operation in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sanxingdui Ruins were discovered accidentally by a farmer; has since been the site of over 60K relics
item.description: Sanxingdui Ruins is an archaeological site in Guanghan City. Official archaeological excavations have been conducted since 1934 at the site after its discovery in 1929.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Wildlife lover facing charges after allegedly assaulting passersby while attempting to capture a baby seagull
item.description: A Belfast woman was in court Tuesday after allegedly attacking passersby who tried to intervene with her capture of a baby seagull in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation
